From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American Negro league baseball team

| Chicago Brown Bombers | |
| --- | --- |
| Information | |
| League | * [Negro Major League](/wiki/Negro_Major_League "Negro Major League") (1942) * [United States League](/wiki/United_States_League "United States League") (1945) |
| Location | [Chicago, Illinois](/wiki/Chicago,_Illinois "Chicago, Illinois") |
| Established | 1940s |
| Disbanded | 1940s |

The **Chicago Brown Bombers** were a semi-pro and [Negro league baseball](/wiki/Negro_league_baseball "Negro league baseball") team in the 1940s. They played in the [Negro Major League](/wiki/Negro_Major_League "Negro Major League") and later, the [United States League](/wiki/United_States_League "United States League").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chicago_Brown_Bombers&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Brown Bombers played in [Minnesota](/wiki/Minnesota "Minnesota") in July, 1942 against the [Twin Cities Gophers](/w/index.php?title=Twin_Cities_Gophers&action=edit&redlink=1 "Twin Cities Gophers (page does not exist)") in Negro Major Baseball League of America games.[[1]](#cite_note-google-1) In 1945, the Brown Bombers were members of The United States League for one season before the league folded.
